3.why: The rotation works on an n by n grid.

4.why: We build the answer in a separate grid first.

5.why: The rotated grid needs n rows.

6.why: Each row must hold n placeholder cells.
6.invariant: Rows fill with zeros before values.

7.why: We copy every source cell to its rotated spot.

8.why: Visit each cell in this row.

9.happening: Send this cell to its rotated position.
9.why: Clockwise rotation sends row r, col c to row c, col n-1-r.
9.invariant: rotated fills with the turned image.

10.why: Now copy the rotated grid back into matrix.

11.why: Copy each cell back in place.
11.invariant: In-place requirement satisfied by copying back.

12.happening: Write the rotated value back into matrix[{row}][{col}].
12.why: The original matrix must hold the result.

init.why: Prepare a fresh n by n matrix.

update.happening: Place a cell into its rotated position.

move.happening: Copy the rotated grid back into matrix.
